subject: Geography
title: Ice Sheets
description: A quick, practical guide to Ice Sheets.

hook.text: Antarctica's ice sheet is so heavy that it pushes the continent's bedrock below sea level.

buildup.text: The ice averages 2.16 km thick and covers 14 million square kilometers.

discovery.text: If all Antarctic ice melted, global sea levels would rise about 58 meters.

twist.text: The Greenland ice sheet holds enough ice to raise seas by 7 meters—and it's melting faster.

climax.text: Earth's ice sheets are the biggest wild card in sea level predictions.

punchline.text: Two ice sheets hold the future of every coastline on Earth.

quiz.question: How much would sea levels rise if all Antarctic ice melted?
